zh.lifeSummary: 當Maud Beauchamp於大約1229年出生在英國貝德福爾貝德福德時，她的父親William de Beauchamp45歲，母親Ida Longespée22歲。她於1257年之前與Roger Mowbray Baron of Thirsk在英國越可爾結婚。他們至少是6個兒子的父母。她去世於1273年4月4日之前在英國英格蘭越可爾霍文加姆，埋葬在英國英格蘭越可爾朋特福拉可特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Maud Beauchamp于大约1229年出生在英国貝德福爾貝德福德时，她的父亲William de Beauchamp45岁，母亲Ida Longespée22岁。她于1257年之前与Roger Mowbray Baron of Thirsk在英国越可爾结婚。他们至少是6个儿子的父母。她去世于1273年4月4日之前在英国英格兰越可爾霍文加姆，埋葬在英国英格兰越可爾朋特福拉可特。

nameMeanings.name: Beauchamp
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Antoine, Jacques, Yves, Andre, Armand, Emile, Fernand, Marcel, Marthe, Remi, Alcide, Camille.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of several places in France, for example in Manche and Somme, that are named with Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + champ(s) ‘field, plain’ (from Latin bello campo ‘beautiful field’). In English the surname is generally pronounced Beecham . Compare Bushaw .
English: in some cases, an Anglo-Norman French adaptation of Scottish Campbell , by translation into French of the folk etymology de campo bello ‘from the fair field’.

variants.name: Beecham
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): variant of   Beauchamp  , reflecting the normal English pronunciation.

variants.name: Beachem
meanings: English (Somerset and Gloucestershire): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Bagent
meanings: possibly a colloquial pronunciation of Beauchamp or Beecham . Old French Beau- often appears as Be(e)- in English surnames. The -ch- has been voiced and excrescent -t has been added.

perhaps an Anglicized form of a French or Flemish surname such as Beaujean and Bejean, from Old French bel Jehan ‘handsome John’ + excrescent -t. It could have been introduced to England in the 15th or 16th century by Flemish migrants.

variants.name: Beacham
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Campbell
meanings: Scottish: nickname from Gaelic cam ‘crooked, bent’ + beul ‘mouth’. As a result of folk etymology, the surname was often represented in Latin documents as de bello campo ‘of the fair field’, which led to the name sometimes being ‘translated’ into Anglo-Norman French as Beauchamp .
Irish (North Armagh): adopted for Gaelic Mac Cathmhaoil ‘son of Cathmhaol’ (literally ‘battle chief’): see Caulfield and Cowell .
English: variant of   Camel  , under the influence of the Scottish name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Bushaw
meanings: Americanized form of French   Beauchamp   and   Bouchard  .

variants.name: Beacom
meanings: English (Somerset) and Irish (Fermanagh): of Norman origin, a variant of   Beauchamp  .

nameMeanings.name: Maud
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Medieval vernacular form of Matilda . This form was characteristically Low German (i.e. including medieval Dutch and Flemish). The wife of William the Conqueror , who bore this name, was the daughter of Baldwin , Count of Flanders. In Flemish and Dutch the letter -t- was generally lost when it occurred between vowels, giving forms such as Ma(h)auld. Maud or Matilda was also the name of the daughter ( 1102–67 ) of Henry I of England; she was married early in life to the Holy Roman Emperor Henry V , and later disputed the throne of England with her cousin Stephen . In 1128 she married Geoffrey , Count of Anjou. A medieval chronicler commented, ‘she was a good woman, but she had little bliss with him’. The name Maud became quite common in England in the 19th century, when its popularity was influenced in part by Tennyson's poem Maud, published in 1855 , but has not been much used since the early decades of the 20th century.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Maud Beauchamp was born about 1229, in Bedford, Bedfordshire, England, her father, William de Beauchamp, was 45 and her mother, Ida Longespée, was 22. She married Roger Mowbray Baron of Thirsk before 1257, in Yorkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 6 sons. She died before 4 April 1273, in Hovingham, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, and was buried in Pontefract, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Maud Beauchamp est née environ 1229, à Bedford, Bedfordshire, Angleterre, son père, William de Beauchamp, avait 45 ans et sa mère, Ida Longespée, avait 22 ans. Elle a épousé Roger Mowbray Baron of Thirsk avant 1257, à Everwyck, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils. Elle est décédée avant 4 avril 1273, à Hovingham, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, et a été enterrée à Pontefract, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Maud Beauchamp um 1229 in Bedford, Bedfordshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William de Beauchamp 45 und ihre Mutter Ida Longespée 22. Sie hat vor 1257 in Yorkshire, England Roger Mowbray Baron of Thirsk geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne. Sie starb vor 4. April 1273 in Hovingham, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Pontefract, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Maud Beauchamp è nata il circa 1229 nel Bedford, Bedfordshire, England, sua padre, William de Beauchamp, aveva 45 anni e sua madre, Ida Longespée, aveva 22 anni. Lei sposato Roger Mowbray Baron of Thirsk prima del 1257, nel Yorkshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli. Morì prima del 4 aprile 1273, nel Hovingham, Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, e fu sepolta a Pontefract, Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Maud Beauchampが約1229年にイギリスベドフォードシュアイアベドフォードで生まれた時，父William de Beauchampは45歳，母Ida Longespéeは22歳であった。1257年以前にイギリスヨークシュアイアでRoger Mowbray Baron of Thirskと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子の親であった。彼女は1273年4月4日以前にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアホーヴィンガムで死去し，イギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアポンテフラクトにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Maud Beauchamp(이)가 대략 1229년 영국 베드퍼드실르 베드퍼드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William de Beauchamp(은)는 45세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Ida Longespée(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 1257년 이전 영국 요르크실르에서 Roger Mowbray Baron of Thirsk(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1273년 4월 4일 이전 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 호빙함에서 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 폰테프라크트에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Maud Beauchamp nasceu aproximadamente 1229, em Bedford, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, William de Beauchamp, tinha 45 anos e sua mãe, Ida Longespée, tinha 22 anos. Ela casou-se com Roger Mowbray Baron of Thirsk antes 1257, em Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos. Ela faleceu antes 4 de abril de 1273, em Hovingham, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, e foi sepultada em Pontefract, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Maud Beauchamp родилась около 1229 г. в местности бедфорд, бедфордшир, Англия, её отцу по имени William de Beauchamp было 45 лет, а матери, которую звали Ida Longespée, 22. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Roger Mowbray Baron of Thirsk перед 1257 г. в местности еркшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей. Она умерла перед 4 апрель 1273 г. в местности ховингхам, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания и была похоронена в местности понтефракт, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания.

Se casó con Roger Mowbray Baron of Thirsk antes de 1257, en Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos. Murió antes de 4 de abril de 1273, en Hovingham, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, y fue sepultada en Pontefract, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
